mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a 3-month decline in policy rates should predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive more often than negative when FEDFUNDS has declined for 3 consecutive months, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL inflation acceleration above the prior 12-month pace raises expected tightening and compresses valuation multiples, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises above its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if weekly SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of months where CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 12-month average, or if the signal's 1-year hit rate falls below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE together with slowing PAYEMS growth indicates labor-market deterioration that weakens household income expectations and risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint UNRATE-PAYEMS condition does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered observations, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in RSAFS signals stronger consumer spending and revenue momentum, which should lift equity earnings expectations and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average after RSAFS growth accelerates for two consecutive months, or if the effect disappears in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while HOUST falls, industrial output is being supported by tightening supply conditions rather than broad demand, which should pressure margins and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO up month-over-month while HOUST down month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the INDPRO/HOUST combination does not precede negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of occurrences, or if the interaction term is insignificant in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp improvement in GDPC1 after a prior slowdown indicates re-acceleration in final demand and supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth turns positive after a prior quarterly deceleration
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns fail to outperform baseline following positive GDPC1 growth inflections, or if the signal reverses sign in more than one rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Stable or falling UNRATE alongside continued PAYEMS gains signals resilient labor income and consumption, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20-day returns are not more frequent when UNRATE is flat-to-down and PAYEMS is rising, or if the combined condition adds no predictive power beyond PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, an elevated FEDFUNDS rate suppresses risk appetite and raises financing costs, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next month once policy is clearly restrictive.
failure_condition: Reject if SPX 20-day forward returns are not negative in at least 55% of months with FEDFUNDS above 4.5%, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for recent CPIAUCSL changes.
